title: search — background jobs
description: Live sync happens via IndexModelJob dispatched from HasSearchable's Eloquent observers (per-model save/delete). Long-running reindex / backfill goes through the orchestrator + shard + coordinator + swap chain, matching the transfer module pipeline. Analytics recording is fire-and-forget via RecordSearchAnalyticsEventJob. Prune runs on schedule.

jobs.purpose: Push one model to its configured search index. Dispatched by HasSearchable's `saved` observer with a 30-second unique window so rapid re-saves collapse. Also called by ReindexModelShardJob for bulk backfill.

Drop a physical engine index. Two triggers: (1) SearchIndex soft-deleted + swap_grace_days elapsed. (2) Post-successful-alias-swap, the previous physical version's grace has elapsed. Also called with force=true from TenantErased cascade to skip the grace.

Top-level orchestrator for a reindex. Creates a fresh empty target index (`{name}_v{target_version}`), enumerates the source records (from live DB or from an XferArtifact per `source` field), decides whether to shard based on `search.shards.enabled` + row count, dispatches N ReindexModelShardJobs via Bus::batch(), and chains ReindexCoordinatorJob + SwapIndexAliasJob + SendSearchSyncNotificationJob on completion.
jobs.notes: tries=1 — shard-level retries handled at the shard job. Retrying the orchestrator would double-process.

jobs.purpose: Runs one shard of a sharded reindex. Reads rows [offset, offset+limit), pushes to the TARGET index (not live). Increments SearchSyncJob shard counters via atomic UPDATE. Fires SearchSyncJobShardCompleted on terminal transition.

Coordinator sitting at the end of the shard batch. Verifies every shard reached terminal, aggregates counters into SearchSyncJob, and decides succeeded vs partially_succeeded vs failed. Fires the matching completion event. Only when status=succeeded does it dispatch SwapIndexAliasJob — partial success or failure means the old alias stays live (rollback-safe).

Atomically repoint `{index_name}_live` from `_v{source_version}` to `_v{target_version}` via the engine adapter's native alias-swap operation. Only runs when the reindex reached status=succeeded. Emits SearchIndexAliasSwapped. Schedules PurgeIndexModelJob for the old version with a `swap_grace_days` delay so rollback is one command.
jobs.notes: Meilisearch: uses UpdateIndex swap. PG-FTS: schema-owned table + view rename atomic via DDL. pgvector: same. Elasticsearch: aliases API.

jobs.purpose: Push the current effective synonym set (system + tenant, filtered by is_active) to every engine's native synonym storage for the given (tenant, language) scope. Rebuilds `search:synonyms:{tenant}:{lang}` cache key. Dispatched from SearchSynonymObserver.created/updated/deleted.
Retention scan. Four passes per run.
steps: PASS 1 (analytics rows): For each retention tier (short/medium/long): DELETE FROM search_analytics_events WHERE retention_tier = tier AND created_at < now() - tier_days LIMIT prune_chunk_size. Loop.

PASS 2 (query text scrub): UPDATE search_analytics_events SET query = NULL WHERE query IS NOT NULL AND created_at < now() - query_text_days. Independent of PASS 1 — GDPR guardrail even when row is retained.
PASS 3 (sync-job rows): For each retention tier: DELETE FROM search_sync_jobs WHERE retention_tier = tier AND created_at < now() - tier_days AND status IN terminal.

PASS 4 (stale queued/running sweep): UPDATE search_sync_jobs SET status='cancelled', cancel_reason='stalled' WHERE status IN ('queued','running') AND last_progress_at < now() - stale_hours. Emits SearchSyncJobCancelled per row.
PASS 5 (soft-deleted hard-delete): Hard-delete SearchIndex + SearchSynonym + SearchSavedQuery rows past their soft-delete grace.
jobs.notes: Runs daily 03:45 UTC. Idempotent — no-op when caught up. Same job also handles --stale-only when invoked from the 15-min sweep.

jobs.purpose: Async insertion of one search_analytics_events row from the search / suggest / click endpoints. Runs off the request thread so latency of the API call is not affected. Fires the matching Query / NoResults / ClickThrough / Suggest event on insert.
jobs.notes: Dedicated `search-analytics` queue so a burst of query volume can't back up sync operations.

jobs.purpose: Fan out the completion / failure notification through the notifications module using search_sync_jobs.notify_channels + notify_locale. Chained after the coordinator terminal transition.

search.description: Reindex + shard + coordinator + swap + sync + synonym cache warm-up. Isolated so a big reindex doesn't stall live save-to-index dispatches (which are also on this queue but capped by shouldBeUnique + short retry backoff).

search-analytics.description: Fire-and-forget analytics recording. Dedicated so a spike in query volume can't back up sync / reindex work.
